riskFactors: Backed states bTokens are no longer available for new issuance, which limits primary-market growth and makes the product more redemption-only for existing holders.
CoinGecko showed $0 24h trading volume and warned that BC3M tokens had stopped trading on listed exchanges, indicating weak observable secondary liquidity.
No bC3M-specific public proof-of-reserves, reserve breakdown, or audit/report URL was confirmed from the accessible official product page.
The token is exposed to issuer/certificate structure risk, service-provider risk, and the underlying Amundi ETF tracking Eurozone short-term government bills.
The product is restricted for U.S. persons and not promoted/offered to UK clients; investor eligibility and jurisdiction restrictions reduce accessibility.
Contract verification status was not confirmed for all listed chain deployments in this pass.
mitigants: The official product page identifies Backed Assets (JE) Limited as issuer and lists the product ISIN, underlying ETF, underlying ISIN, service providers, custodian entities, and security agent.
Backed's legal documentation page states its Base Prospectus was approved in Liechtenstein by the FMA as of May 8, 2026, with Swiss law as applicable law for products.
The underlying exposure is a short-duration Eurozone government bill ETF rather than a long-duration or unsecured private-credit asset.
Backed states redemption remains fully supported for existing bToken holders even though new issuance is closed.
The product has a clear official product page and public legal documentation hub.
